tagline: Ebola has been building for months, Outside forces have made it far worse.

hero.title: Minerals, deportations, and medicine. Inside Trump’s bilateral health agreements.
hero.author: Celine Gounder, MD
hero.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
hero.excerpt: The Trump administration signed bilateral health deals with over 30 countries. Public records show who donated, who benefited, and what happened to countries that refused.
hero.brief:
Trump's Graft and Extortion Racquet Comes for Public Health
-Trump signed bilateral health deals with 32 countries, worth $20.3B.
-Deals tied to tariffs, deportations & mineral access.
-Inaugural donors hold business in recipient nations.
-Countries that refused lost funding.

selected.title: This new OMB Rule Is Bigger Than Science. Much Bigger.
selected.author: Elizabeth Ginexi, PhD
selected.excerpt: This image was generated by Google AI (Gemini)
selected.brief:
-OMB's proposed rewrite of 2 CFR Part 200 covers ALL federal grants, not just science.
-$1.1T in state/local funding at risk: Medicaid, transit, education, food aid.
-Comment by July 13.

selected.title: Summary of Key Changes in OMB’s Proposed Federal Financial Assistance Rule
selected.author: Elizabeth Ginexi, PhD
selected.excerpt: Russell Vought is going to destroy American Science
selected.brief:
Politics Trumps Science
-OMB's proposed rule puts political appointees in charge of every federal grant.
-Peer review made advisory only.
-Active grants can be terminated anytime.
-Conferences, journals, publishing all require pre-approval.

selected.title: Scoop: HHS asks confined hantavirus cruise passengers to assist in propaganda.
selected.author: Jeremy Faust, MD
selected.excerpt: The US Department of Health and Human Services invited the Andes hantavirus-exposed former MV Hondius cruise ship passengers staying in a National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska to participate in some free government propaganda.
selected.brief:
From Lockdown Hate to Quarantine Lovefest
-HHS asked hantavirus-exposed MV Hondius passengers in Nebraska quarantine to share "fun" room photos.
-Passengers still face 42-day monitoring.
-Stunt undermines the seriousness of biosecurity.

recent.title: In Animal Study, Nanobots Repair Spinal Cords
recent.author: Steven Novella, MD
recent.excerpt: For my entire career as a neurologist the ability to repair an injured spinal cord has been one of the holy grails. There has always been promising new research that definitely increases our knowledge but doesn’t lead to an effective treatment. recent.title: The $2.5 Billion Reason Kenya Can't Say No
recent.author: Celine Gounder, MD
recent.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
recent.excerpt: A Kenyan court blocked the Ebola quarantine facility. The government defied the order. Protesters marched. Here's where things stand.

recent.title: Behind every vaccine question is a person
recent.author: Jess Steier, DrPH
recent.excerpt: After six years of listening to questions, doubts, and pushback, this is where our thinking has landed so far.

recent.title: Injectable Peptides – The New Snake Oil
recent.author: Steven Novella, MD
recent.excerpt: We are going backwards. Hopefully this will be temporary trend, but it has been consistent for the past few decades. Prior to the FDA we had the “wild west” of patent medicines – anyone could put anything in a bottle and sell it with any claims. It was up to the average person to decide if a product was safe or effecti
recent.brief:
•	Peptides, microproteins pushed by wellness industry
•	Loophole allows sale without FDA approval 
•	RFK Jr uses them and is pushing approval of 7 
•	Top seller BPC-157 has just 1 unblinded 12-person trial

recent.title: Keeping Ebola Out of America, Whatever the Cost to Americans
recent.author: Craig Spencer MD
recent.excerpt: The White House is abdicating its responsibility to our own by sending Americans exposed to or infected with Ebola to Kenya rather than bringing them home
